changes_made: Changed k8s_rbac_configuration_change_monitoring to k8s_rbac_configuration_change_audit for clarity
review_notes: The functions are generally well-mapped to the NIST control CM-6-d, focusing on monitoring and controlling configuration changes. However, the naming of the RBAC function could be improved for clarity and consistency with the audit focus.
naming_improvements: Consider using 'audit' instead of 'monitoring' for RBAC to align with audit logging focus
title: Configuration Settings - Monitor and control changes to...
step1_reasoning: The control CM-6-d focuses on monitoring and controlling configuration changes. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that configuration changes are logged (audit logging), preventing unauthorized configuration changes (admission controllers), and monitoring access to configuration settings (RBAC). These functions ensure that any changes to the Kubernetes configuration are tracked and controlled.
